name: restraint — no @mention because none truly fit (should score HIGH)

input.name: James
post: Shipped a since-last-post window and an in-app cohort feed.

Wins — James shipped two things on router-daybook today. The daily digest now pulls work since his last Router post instead of a fixed midnight-to-midnight day, anchored to the post's feed timestamp so it stays correct on any machine. He also added an in-app feed view that shows his posts and the cohort's in one list, newest first, instead of only a button that kicked you out to the browser.

Struggles — The window math fought him. A missing checkpoint, a stale one, and a future timestamp from clock skew all had to clamp to a seven-day floor, and Codex stores sessions in per-day directories, so the collector had to walk each day in the range rather than do one bounds check. The in-app feed also needed a small view-state tracker so the back button returns to a sane screen.

Insight — Deriving the window's anchor from data that already exists — the Router's own record of your posts — beats writing a local checkpoint file that starts empty. The feed is the source of truth, so it just works on a fresh install.

Asking — James wants the since-last-post anchor to survive clock skew across machines. He is asking whether the Router entries endpoint exposes a server-authoritative post timestamp he can read instead of the local clock, and what precision it reports.

feedEntries.content: Tuning a RAG pipeline for our docs search; chunking strategy is killing recall.
Shipped a Stripe billing portal integration today, webhooks were the tricky part.
Training a small classifier to route support tickets, label noise is rough.

input.workLog: router-daybook: since-last-post window anchored to feed timestamp, 7-day clamp for missing/stale/future checkpoint, codex per-day walk; in-app feed view (inline + persistent) with currentView() tracker and return-target guard. Files: transcripts.js, router.js, main.js, app.js.
